title: The Strait That Isn't: How Iranian 'Shipping Management' Collapsed Real Traffic by 90%+ While Claiming Control
narrative:
**Adversarial Analysis | May 17, 2026**

Al Jazeera reports Iran is "managing shipping" through the Strait of Hormuz, framing its presence as orderly control of a strategic waterway. Iranian officials claim commercial traffic is functioning normally under their oversight. Pentagon and US State Department statements say the strait remains "open" while deploying additional carrier groups.

The physical evidence tells a different story.
telemetry: AIS satellite data: shipping traffic collapsed 87-97% since February 2026
UK Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO): "extremely limited" commercial traffic — 3 confirmed transits
62 vessels identified exhibiting AIS spoofing — false positions transmitted
Multiple tanker attacks documented May 2026; US Navy engaged Iranian tankers May 8
LinkedIn analysis: AIS data may be "missing half the picture" — actual traffic could be lower
Insurance premiums for Hormuz transit reportedly up 400%+
analysis:
The gap between Tehran's framing and the AIS data is the entire story. From roughly 175 daily transits to under two dozen. From "commercially functioning" to "extremely limited" in the words of the UK's own maritime operations office.

The 62 vessels running AIS spoofing is the detail that makes this worse, not better. Ships are deliberately concealing their positions — which means the 6-22 daily transits visible on AIS may be only the traffic willing to show itself. The actual number could be lower. Insurance premiums spiking 400% tells you what the market thinks about the risk.

The US says the strait is "open" while routing carrier groups through it. That's a verbal hedge. Calling something open doesn't make it function as an open waterway. The carrier presence is itself evidence that the strait is not operating normally. You don't need a carrier group to protect routine commercial traffic.

The 87-97% traffic collapse is the number that matters. Everything else — the diplomatic language, the "open" claims, the carrier deployments — is spin layered on top of a physical fact.
verdict: [SIPHONED VERDICT]: Iran says it's managing shipping. The data says 87-97% of commercial traffic vanished. Six to 22 ships a day instead of 175. AIS spoofing hides even those numbers. The "open" strait is a press release. The collapse is the policy.
